user_query:
I need a repeatable system status inspection and a faculty update package I can send to our department head. Please use the two input files at input/last_month_summary.md and input/department_update_draft.md, inspect the current system, and produce the following deliverables under outputs/:

1) outputs/system_status.json — A structured JSON snapshot of the current system with these fields:
   - timestamp_iso: ISO 8601 timestamp of the inspection
   - host: hostname
   - disk: object for root (/) containing total_gb, used_gb, used_percent
   - memory: object containing total_mb, used_mb, available_mb, swap_total_mb, swap_used_mb
   - load: object containing one_min, five_min, fifteen_min
   - systemd: object containing failed_units_count and failed_units (array of unit names); if systemctl is unavailable, include {"not_available": true}
   - kernel_errors: object with recent_count and top_keywords (array of up to 3 {keyword, count}); if logs are unavailable, include {"not_available": true}
   - python: object containing version (e.g., "3.10.12")
   - errors: object containing command_failures (array of {cmd, stderr_snippet}) for any command that fails, otherwise an empty array

2) outputs/system_status_report.md — A brief human-readable report that:
   - Summarizes the same metrics as in the JSON
   - Includes an "Action Notes" section constructed by these rules: if disk.used_percent >= 85, add a note to clean up disk space; if systemd.failed_units_count > 0, add a note to investigate/restart listed units; if kernel_errors.recent_count > 0, add a note to review kernel errors; if none of the above apply, say "No immediate action"
   - Includes a "Changes since last month" section computing differences vs input/last_month_summary.md for: disk used percent (percentage points), memory used MB, failed units count, and kernel error count

3) Edit input/department_update_draft.md by replacing only the placeholders listed below, then save the revised copy to outputs/department_update_final.md. Keep all other text unchanged. Replace these placeholders with values derived from your inspection and the previous month file:
   - {{DATE}}: timestamp_iso
   - {{HOST}}: hostname
   - {{DISK_USED_GB}} and {{DISK_TOTAL_GB}}: numbers from current disk root (/) in GB
   - {{DISK_USED_PERCENT}}: current root (/) used percent
   - {{MEM_USED_MB}} and {{MEM_TOTAL_MB}}: current memory used and total MB
   - {{SWAP_USED_MB}}: current swap used MB
   - {{LOAD_1}}, {{LOAD_5}}, {{LOAD_15}}: load averages
   - {{FAILED_UNITS_SUMMARY}}: if supported, list names of failed units or "none"
   - {{ERRORS_SUMMARY}}: e.g., "N errors; top keywords: k1 (c1), k2 (c2), k3 (c3)" or "none"
   - {{PYTHON_VERSION}}: current Python3 version
   - {{DISK_DELTA_PERCENT}}: current used_percent minus last month used percent (percentage points)
   - {{MEM_DELTA_MB}}: current memory used MB minus last month memory used MB
   - {{FAILED_UNITS_DELTA}}: current failed units count minus last month count
   - {{ERROR_COUNT_DELTA}}: current kernel error count minus last month count
   - {{ACTION_NOTES}}: the same notes as in the report, condensed to a single paragraph

Constraints and guidance:
- Use only local commands (no Internet). If a command is unavailable or fails, record that in system_status.json under errors.command_failures and proceed by marking the relevant field as not_available.
- You may use typical shell commands for measurement (e.g., df -h for disk, free -m or vm_stat for memory, uptime or similar for load). For failed units, use systemctl --failed if available. For kernel errors, scan either dmesg or journalctl -p err; count error lines in a recent window (e.g., last 500 lines) and compute top keywords by lowercasing and counting non-trivial tokens; list up to three keywords.
- Parse input/last_month_summary.md to extract last month values. Compute the deltas numerically and include them in both outputs/system_status_report.md and outputs/department_update_final.md.
- Do not hardcode values. Reflect the current system state at the time you run.
- Ensure all three deliverables are created at the specified paths.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
